mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when retail sales growth decouples from housing starts, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses during supply-constrained periods where industrial output rises but input costs outpace pricing power, resulting in negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 5-day forward returns, with a mean directional accuracy below 50% across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 5 percent threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite CPI exceeding 5 percent, or the negative signal is neutralized by concurrent fiscal stimulus announcements.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance when GDP growth is driven solely by government spending rather than private consumption.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its recent trend signals manufacturing contraction and weakening business investment, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial declines are offset by strong services sector growth, resulting in positive equity performance.
